12:31 p.m.
Anyone ever looked on 433?
Dayle - M0OUE 2025-08-12 2:28 p.m.
Not on MeshCore, but LoRa APRS (439mhz). This was a trip to Llandudno, each icon is a station which received me either directly or indirectly
M29 2025-08-12 3:39 p.m.
What's this Dayle? Looks brill
M29
What's this Dayle? Looks brill
Dayle - M0OUE 2025-08-12 4:27 p.m.
APRS is an amateur radio system for sharing information about an area, for example position of people and data from weather stations. It also does (basic) messaging. It's been going since the 90s but more recently a LoRa based version has been made which links in with the old system. The image above shows all the stations which picked up my car as I drove to Llandudno and back last month. They either picked me up directly, or received the packet from another station. The thing with LoRa APRS is it's very slow. Good at getting a signal out but not so much for two way communication. (edited)
1
4:29 p.m.
It uses the 70cm amateur band which is much lower than the 868mhz we have been using, meaning it generally goes further. (edited)
1

DementedManc

Dayle - M0OUE 2025-08-24 3:40 p.m.

1
DementedManc 2025-08-24 3:42 p.m.

Dayle - M0OUE 2025-08-24 3:47 p.m.
That'll be your issue then, ideally an external antenna would be used, but you can also get away with one in the loft (I'm using a dipole on the loft not even designed for 868mhz)

Eccles 2025-08-24 4:28 p.m.
Unlike meshtastic, the companion nodes don't relay, so you need line of sight to a repeater, which from your earlier messages with Dayle puts you on the fringe. G5TAR - Mike 2025-08-29 6:23 p.m.
@Dayle - M0OUE just thinking on the filter front, all the affordable cavities will be tuned for helium miners won’t they?
6:24 p.m.

G5TAR - Mike
@Dayle - M0OUE just thinking on the filter front, all the affordable cavities will be tuned for helium miners won’t they?
Dayle - M0OUE 2025-08-29 6:25 p.m.
We've just been speaking about it, turns out the repeater already has a cavity filter, but it's one of the older G2 stations with the poor RX. I've suggested swapping it out for something else to see if that helps
6:26 p.m.
(also you can sometimes get the manufacturer to adjust the cavity filter)
G5TAR - Mike 2025-08-29 6:28 p.m.
Ahh okay gotcha. Yeah be interesting to see what a different node does. Must be some setup that with a G2 and a cavity.
6:30 p.m.
We still plan on deploying a repeater closer to you guys at some point too when time allows so don’t sink a load of money into that one repeater just in case it’s not the silver bullet
6:33 p.m.
We look to be receiving you guys fairly reliably anyway so hopefully it’s not just favourable band conditions and we do have a viable path across

@M29 just seen your message about firmware upgrades. What board are you running? Some of them you can update OTA so you don’t have to fetch the node down

SCCS 2025-08-31 5:15 p.m.

Dayle - M0OUE 2025-08-31 5:34 p.m.

Duffer 2025-09-01 6:14 p.m.

Dayle - M0OUE 2025-09-02 2:14 a.m.
Not gonna lie this thing is pretty cool
drewzh 2025-09-02 2:55 a.m.
Looks nice. Any gripes? I’m looking at this or the new cardputer
Dayle - M0OUE 2025-09-02 3:06 a.m.
Gripes are mainly software stuff. Other than that, it's a little thicker than I was expecting. The display is unprotected and picks up fingerprints the keyboard functionally works but you wouldn't want to write anything long on it. The antennas are flimsy and flip out easily. It boils down to it being a development device pretending to be a consumer device, and suffers because of it. I think I would buy it again if I had to though, it's fun.
drewzh 2025-09-02 4:29 a.m.
Nice one for that - really appreciate it. Hard to find honest opinions these days. I still think it’s a better suited device than the tdeck in my opinion. I actually find those kinds of keys easier to type on than the tdeck. I have a cardputer and find that easier also

4:04 p.m.
The fact is I can contact it from my living room and the repeater is at the top of a hill 5 miles away, so I'm pretty sure the antenna is still up and running. 4:05 p.m.

4:05 p.m.
I'm still using the 90degree elbow rubber ducky the solar node came with
4:06 p.m.

Dayle - M0OUE 2025-09-02 4:15 p.m.

5:18 p.m.

M29 2025-09-02 5:27 p.m.
That sucks dude do you not fancy doubling up on adapters? RP-SMA to SMA then SMA to N? drewzh 2025-09-02 5:31 p.m.

drewzh 2025-09-04 4:00 a.m.
I'm going to add a laminated card to my remote but rather public node (courtesy of GPT) Hello! This small device is part of a community wireless project called MeshCore. It helps provide free, low-power long-range connectivity for sensors and messages in the local area. I’ve placed it here temporarily because the location is ideal for coverage. It doesn’t damage the land, doesn’t use much power (less than a mobile phone charger), and is safe for people, animals, and the environment. If you are the landowner, I’d really like to hear from you. If you’d prefer I remove it, please just let me know and I’ll do so right away. If you’re curious, I’d also be happy to explain more about the project — you may even find it useful yourself! Please feel free to drop me a quick message at: [your email / anonymous address] Thank you for your understanding, A local MeshCore community member

drewzh 2025-09-04 10:15 a.m.
I'm not so precious about what others do honestly - if they want to break the law, then whatever - unless it affects me directly, then they can do whatever they want. 1
Dayle - M0OUE 2025-09-04 10:29 a.m.
I too don't have an issue with using too much power, OFCOM have basically said this region of the band is a wild west which they will not regulate. I do have issues when the decision to use more power negatively affects a large part of the country, and benefits no one. One way links are something we should all be actively working to fix, either by deploying more repeaters to fill in problem areas, or changing repeaters when they have known RX issues (eg the G2 Station) We all want the same thing in the end, which is a usable mesh for everyone.

drewzh 2025-09-05 5:48 a.m.
Yeah that's just a pre-solar version. I have few at multiple stages of development. I 3D print a PETG wedge that angles the panel and then silicone this to the door and then silicone the panel to the wedge. I then feet the solar cable down, under the wedge and then back up into the case and seal with silicone
5:49 a.m.
5:50 a.m.
5:51 a.m.
5:52 a.m.
RE your rubber gasket btw - I wouldn't trust that unless that part is underside and not exposed too much (even then, it can technically 'wick' up due to pressure flucuations - an auto vent helps with this)
SCCS 2025-09-05 5:53 a.m.
My pole set up is janky as it is
1
drewzh 2025-09-05 5:57 a.m.
Nice. Few tips (I've been building/hacking outdoor things for a while now so hope you don't mind) - run your self sealing tape from the bottom up, it creates an overlapping joint that sheds water, similar to tiles on a roof. Also, you'd be surprised how much dirt will collect on a flat mounted panel like that (I assume you're in between houses which is why it's angled up) - it's really best to angle it roughly 40 degrees if you can, or at least angled slightly, as it will not only have significant gains in power absorption, but it'll keep it much cleaner long term.
5:59 a.m.
If anybody is interested in those panels I'm using btw, they're very good (kept batteries charged all year round), keep clean and are relatively easy to work with - https://www.amazon.co.uk/dp/B09Q87WKGR?ref_=ppx_hzsearch_conn_dt_b_fed_asin_title_1&th=1

G5TAR - Mike 2025-09-05 9:01 p.m.
@SteBurneyM41-DDC we saw your messages around stacking attenuators on the G2 - isn’t that just making the already poor Rx even worse? It’s not working either as we’re still receiving all the flood packets from you guys. Anyways I’ve had to disable HX2 tonight as so many of our guys were complaining - which has resulted in some of our South Yorkshire guys becoming isolated from the mesh. HX2 (the repeater you guys are flooding) is a remote solar repeater running legal power, a 40 minute drive away from where I live, on private farmland where I got the land owner to agree to host the node. There’s nothing i can physically do to try and prevent your traffic bridging across as I’ll just end up winding the land owner up to the point where he makes me take the node down. So I’ve just had to switch it off

Mystic 2025-09-06 7:13 a.m.
should certainly be possible - messages start with a header describing their type then a list of all repeater id it passed through so shouldn't be too difficult to have a list of disallowed repeater ids from which packets are dropped. you could theoretically put a hardcoded if (pkt->path_len != 0 && pkt->path[pkt->path_len - 1] == 0x02) { _mgr->free(pkt); return; }, but might be better to wait for it to be implemented as an actual feature (edited)

M29 2025-09-11 3:41 a.m.

M29 2025-09-11 9:09 a.m.

9:12 a.m.

9:13 a.m.

9:13 a.m.

M29
No it really wouldn't dude. Nothing wrong at all with Winter Hills TX either it's an absolute beast. It would however help your RX. But obviously I'm wrong and Andy Kirby
drewzh 2025-09-11 9:24 a.m.
I never said there was anything up with Winter Hill either - that's not the issue - my RX is
M29 2025-09-11 9:31 a.m.
Ohh I know your joking dude no fear there but increasing it does genuinely help your RX and it may not fix your NF but it can help suck in weaker signals more robust through it especially at longer range deployments. Regarding WH I thought you meant he needed to up TX that's how I read it man. It's your baby mate you do what's good for you I'm only sticking my nose in
drewzh 2025-09-11 9:53 a.m.
From what I'm told and from some research into the coding rate - it's the TX side that decides the error correction to apply to the packet. So it wouldn't matter if I upped the CR on my side, as others are hearing my repeater loud and clear (including winter hill). Where I fall short is hearing WH... which means the only way CR would help me is if they upped theres.... but as I said in my first message, that's obviously not what we want, so I'm looking into my noise floor (edited)
9:55 a.m.
You said upping my CR would help my RX - I am intrigued how that would work? (edited)
M29 2025-09-11 10:23 a.m.
@drewzh (edited)
drewzh 2025-09-11 10:29 a.m.
10:30 a.m.
I can get ChatGPT to confess to the murder of JFK
10:31 a.m.
Besides your question wasn’t precise enough - it may have confused RX performance of the mesh in general or the receiving end of your packet. The truth is, it will not improve reception at the CR-upping source
M29 2025-09-11 10:40 a.m.
Everything I'm reading says it will? It will help decode more of the packet headers leading to better RX? Only negative is a higher duty cycle and slightly longer time to albeit in milliseconds TX RX
10:42 a.m.
It's giving me charts and everything here
10:42 a.m.
All in favour
10:43 a.m.
Listen Dude I'm saying yes your saying no. Let's leave it at that
drewzh 2025-09-11 10:45 a.m.
The CR is specified on an outgoing packet header and ups the error correction on the receiving end. It has no bearing on incoming packets. There’s really no 2 ways about it
2
M29
Everything I'm reading says it will? It will help decode more of the packet headers leading to better RX? Only negative is a higher duty cycle and slightly longer time to albeit in milliseconds TX RX
drewzh 2025-09-11 10:45 a.m.
Would love to see where
10:46 a.m.
We’re all learning - if I don’t understand something then I want to be corrected
M29
Everything I'm reading says it will? It will help decode more of the packet headers leading to better RX? Only negative is a higher duty cycle and slightly longer time to albeit in milliseconds TX RX
drewzh 2025-09-11 10:47 a.m.

Mystic 2025-09-11 12:08 p.m.
if i do have to ask chatgpt for something, i tend to phrase it neutrally rather than asking it if it agrees with my side. e.g., in this case, something like 'what does increasing the LoRa CR do in the context of a repeater on meshcore/meshtastic'. otherwise, its more likely to agree with what you're proposing
1
ret 2025-09-11 12:09 p.m.
I do love it for generating HTML & CSS. The best thing about that is it's instantly visually verifyable. No dodgy subtle bugs. You can just look at it and go "yep, that's correct" or "no that's wrong".

I won't let it near embedded C though
drewzh 2025-09-11 12:09 p.m.

ret 2025-09-11 12:10 p.m.

Brins0 2025-09-16 6:00 p.m.

drewzh 2025-09-16 6:24 p.m.
You've not got solar panels have you? They can cause a lot of RF interference (almost too obvious though I know)
6:24 p.m.
Or maybe a terrestrial TV antenna that's attenuating things?
Brins0 2025-09-16 6:30 p.m.
I don't have solar panels, but there's an unplugged TV antenna up there
drewzh 2025-09-16 6:31 p.m.
Even unplugged it can attenuate the signal.... it basically absorbs RF like a sponge.... if it's a considerable distance it's less likely, but if for e.g it in the path of the signal to the nearest hilltop node - that could cause it
Brins0 2025-09-16 7:10 p.m.
I should remove it really anyway, it takes up a load of space and I don't watch TV anyway, it's all garbage and I don't have a TV license anyway
Brins0

drewzh 2025-09-17 6:29 a.m.
Nice - I used to live in bury for a few years (tottington road + rochdale road). What is your local repeater? Winter hill? It probably doesn't matter much if you're getting good SNR from the repeater, but ideally if you can move it away from the wood, that would be ideal (it'll soak up some radiation but not as much as metal). What I've done is screw a small piece of timber to the joist and then mount the antenna to the top of that.
2
ret 2025-09-17 6:37 a.m.

6:43 a.m.

7:11 a.m.

ret 2025-09-17 7:11 a.m.

7:11 a.m.

drewzh 2025-09-17 7:11 a.m.

ret 2025-09-17 7:12 a.m.

ret
The ESP ones... not worth bothering in my experience.
drewzh 2025-09-17 7:12 a.m.
With meshcore I get 2 days ish on the heltec on a single 18650
7:12 a.m.
big difference in power consumption compared to meshtastic as they transmit really infrequently
ret 2025-09-17 7:13 a.m.
Yeah. My understanding is that regular nodes don't transmit unless you're actually sending something (messages etc) or they are adverting (on request)?
ret
Huh. I have PoE available in the loft actually. That's a thought.
drewzh 2025-09-17 7:13 a.m.
I have the RAK ethernet + PoE boards to achieve this, but you can always just use a PoE to USB-C injector
7:13 a.m.
I had working MQTT and TCP control on meshtastic with my setup though so it made sense at the time
7:13 a.m.
Meshcore devs have said they're not going to look into ethernet control on the RAKs
7:14 a.m.
(I raised a ticket and got shut down :D)
ret 2025-09-17 7:14 a.m.
Ah :(
drewzh 2025-09-17 7:14 a.m.
Mayb when I find time in future I can work on adding it myself (I'm a software dev).... just spare time is non existant with work/kids
ret
Yeah. My understanding is that regular nodes don't transmit unless you're actually sending something (messages etc) or they are adverting (on request)?
drewzh 2025-09-17 7:15 a.m.
Advertise on request, but also default to advertising every 3 hours I believe
7:16 a.m.
But yeah - no constant pings of telemetry and relaying other peoples telemetry... it's a much better system in my opnion
ret 2025-09-17 7:16 a.m.
Being able to run a node under Linux would be nice. Something like RFM75 LoRa module & Raspberry Pi. Not sure if that's possible.
7:16 a.m.
Yeah the telemetry stuff on Meshtastic was ridiculous.
7:16 a.m.
So much spam.
drewzh 2025-09-17 7:16 a.m.
meshtastic = great for small local meshes that are independant of any kind of infrastructure (hikers etc)... but for 95% of what people want, Meshcore is a better system as it's far more efficient
ret
Being able to run a node under Linux would be nice. Something like RFM75 LoRa module & Raspberry Pi. Not sure if that's possible.
drewzh 2025-09-17 7:17 a.m.
I'm already doing it via home assistant. I can set automations, track stats of repeaters and plot on graphs, receive and send messages etc - it's awesome
ret 2025-09-17 7:18 a.m.
Ah neat
drewzh 2025-09-17 7:19 a.m.
That said - would be nice to have things decoupled so I didn't have to plug in a USB node to the hass server - if they supported say MQTT or TCP control, I could just orchestrate from the Unraid/hass server and control the node in my attic
7:20 a.m.
I don't think we're going to get that any time soon though unless someone is feeling adventurous as they're mostly just focusing on the core of the system and ignoring (non-mesh or internet connected) sorts of things, which is understantable
ret 2025-09-17 7:23 a.m.
Yeah probably sensible. It's a cool project, I wish I had time to hack on it a bit.

Yisarus 2025-09-21 3:03 p.m.
Hey there, I'm curious what hardware you are using for the Winter Hill South repeater as I am able to hear adverts and messages being repeated by it on my loft repeater way down in Crewe. I can hear but can't reply as I think my repeater is either too quiet or it's just being muffled in my attic. Still, very impressed that I can hear it directly.
Brins0 2025-09-21 3:04 p.m.
Yeah, I'd really like to know too tbh. I presume it's outdoors given the list you've shown in the past having very high RSSIs
Yisarus 2025-09-21 3:05 p.m.
56.16 km away and being heard by a loft repeater is very impressive (edited)
drewzh 2025-09-21 3:06 p.m.

Yisarus 2025-09-21 3:06 p.m.
Yeah, I'm still pretty new to all this. The distance is just blowing my mind
1
Yisarus
Hey there, I'm curious what hardware you are using for the Winter Hill South repeater as I am able to hear adverts and messages being repeated by it on my loft repeater way down in Crewe. I can hear but can't reply as I think my repeater is either too quiet or it's just being muffled in my attic. Still, very impressed that I can hear it directly.
Dayle - M0OUE 2025-09-21 3:07 p.m.
Hey, it's a RAK4631 with a Paradar 6dBi antenna. It's solar powered and further up the hill than it is in the map. Furthest I've got it was Llandudno / Great Orme and that was on a smaller antenna
1
drewzh 2025-09-21 3:07 p.m.
It’s the way the chirps work - it’s quite a neat encoding under the hood
1

Dayle - M0OUE 2025-09-22 12:12 p.m.
Forwarded
SO... CALLING ALL UK users ... can I propose a wider test of Narrow using the Slovakian radio preset, its legal and should help with poor links. The actual radio settings are: 869.618 MHz Bandwidth: 62.5 KHz Spreading Factor: 8 Coding Rate: 8 For companions using Liam's app it's as easy as selecting the Slovakian radio preset.
Originally sent: 2025-09-22 12:06 p.m.

5:14 p.m.
@Brins0
Brins0 2025-09-24 5:15 p.m.
in ha's "actions" thingy, you need to do: action: meshcore.execute_command data: command: set_radio 869.618 62.5 8 8 entry_id: <your entry id>
drewzh 2025-09-24 5:15 p.m.
ah nice one - I've set myself up a UI/dashboard for meshcore

However, I've purchased a "Bingfu 868 Mhz LoRa Antenna 5dBi SMA Male" not realising the SMA on the Tracker L1 is Reverse SMA. Can I just get a sma to reverse sma adapter and I can connect the aerial? 5:40 a.m.

drewzh 2025-09-26 5:41 a.m.

5:47 a.m.

drewzh 2025-09-28 2:46 p.m.

drewzh 2025-09-29 9:20 a.m.

Brins0 2025-09-29 10:31 a.m.

silverphish 🌈
The heltec v4 might be a solar candidate. Theres two versions
Dayle - M0OUE 2025-09-29 10:32 a.m.

Brins0

Dayle - M0OUE 2025-09-29 10:34 a.m.

Brins0 2025-09-29 10:36 a.m.
Ah, it's the underlying chip that's the issue, gotcha
10:37 a.m.
I did notice the esp32-s3 units I have do run rather warm
10:37 a.m.
Disabling the WiFi/Bluetooth does cut power usage down significantly
10:37 a.m.
So perhaps for a repeater with no BT/WiFi it might be ok?
Dayle - M0OUE 2025-09-29 11:04 a.m.
I've not ran the numbers personally (you need a fancy Nordic tool to even get accurate power usage from nRF boards) but the esp32 based boards can be as much as 10x more power hungry than NRF52840 based ones. You're talking hours Vs weeks on a battery
likkleidiot_nwo 2025-09-29 11:06 a.m.

likkleidiot_nwo 2025-10-01 6:57 a.m.
I got sick of my heltec V3 hanging from wires so decided just to create a mounting carrier board and with no knowledge got chat GPT help ( and send me down some money wasting paths..lol) start adding bits and got some guy on Fiverr to do me the schematic for £20..V3 now has a adc, Uart, I2c, solar and battery(CN3791), battery protection and .with a nice power switch thrown in...Then they bring out the V4 ..lmao

likkleidiot_nwo 2025-10-01 2:28 p.m.
I found my issue..my crows nest V3 carrier board is designed for Meshtastic..which for some unknown reason Meshcore have decided to use Different gpio pins for the SDA and SCL..so wouldn't pick it up..just doing a version that should work with pins 17/18 and not 21/22
Yisarus
My connection to Crow's Nest 1 is much better now. Maybe now I won't miss as many messages

Visual Electric
How LoRa Modulation really works - long range communication using c...
LoRa bitrate calculator and understanding LoRa parameters
It can be pretty useful to know how LoRa parameters influence data rate and range, for example when configuring your RNode. In this post I'll go over the basic concepts needed to understand the different LoRa parameters, and how they affect range and data rate.

Yisarus 2025-10-03 11:30 a.m.
Best way to see if there are any repeaters in line of sight is to use the web app (you don't need a companion connected) click on the 3 dots to the top right and click on Tools. Then select Line of Sight, From there you can find where you are on the map, left click and hold to place a marker for your repeater, then do the same for locations of other repeaters to drop another marker. This will then do a line of sight calculation and show you if any terrain is blocking the path. https://app.meshcore.nz/ (edited)
MeshCore
The MeshCore Companion App

Tharsis 2025-10-04 10:07 a.m.
Just want to sanity check myself. I know I'm not exactly in the zone of this region (I'm in the Chorley area), but was wondering if anyone can see any repeaters near Chorley/Preston? I set up a MeshCore node (And a Meshtastic one), and while the MT node has found a good 60/70 nodes stretching from Lancaster to North Wales and plenty in Manchester, the MC node hasn't found a single other node. I'm currently configured using the EU/UK Narrow present after recently discovering that everyone in the UK has apparently switched to that.
Tharsis
Just want to sanity check myself. I know I'm not exactly in the zone of this region (I'm in the Chorley area), but was wondering if anyone can see any repeaters near Chorley/Preston? I set up a MeshCore node (And a Meshtastic one), and while the MT node has found a good 60/70 nodes stretching from Lancaster to North Wales and plenty in Manchester, the MC node hasn't found a single other node. 10:19 a.m.

Dayle - M0OUE 2025-10-04 10:41 a.m.
I think you're in a bit of an awkward place to pick up the rest of the mesh right now, you're a bit north to pick up the Winter Hill South repeater, and probably blocked by winter hill to pick up the Pennine repeaters. In the next few weeks I'll be placing one in the Welsh hills which may help
Dayle - M0OUE
I think you're in a bit of an awkward place to pick up the rest of the mesh right now, you're a bit north to pick up the Winter Hill South repeater, and probably blocked by winter hill to pick up the Pennine repeaters. In the next few weeks I'll be placing one in the Welsh hills which may help
Tharsis 2025-10-04 10:54 a.m.
Thanks for the info. I suspected that might be the case. Going by the MeshCore map, most nearby nodes were last seen back in July (And most earlier), so I had no idea if they were still live or not. I'll keep my node live for the foreseeable future regardless, and maybe some of the MT guys near me might add MC nodes as it continues to pick up momentum. I'll also keep an eye out for your Welsh repeater, as my MT node is definitely picking up more to the west of me and I've gotten good reception for Liverpool and north Wales. (edited)

likkleidiot_nwo 2025-10-08 7:03 p.m.
Just been doing some power test at ideal for the Heltec V3, Xiao Esp32 S3 and Xiao nRF52840 All running Meshcore Bluetooth companion. I know the V3 is power hungry but didn't realise just how much. The Xiao esp32 S3 and the V3 both use the same chips but vast difference in power usage. Be interesting to test the V4 once it arrives This is based on a 5000mah lipo for the estimated runtimes.Test were done with USB for power consumption Inputs used (idle): Heltec V3: 4.761 V, 0.612 A → 2.916 W XIAO ESP32-S3: 4.947 V, 0.108 A → 0.537 W XIAO nRF52840: 4.980 V, 0.017 A → 0.084 W Battery-only runtime (ideal): Heltec V3: 6.34 h XIAO ESP32-S3: 34.45 h XIAO nRF52840: 220.24 h (≈9.18 days) (edited)
1

likkleidiot_nwo 2025-10-09 7:49 a.m.
Are you planning on having the V3 running as a solar repeater..just needs a lot of kit as it's a hungry beast
7:51 a.m.
Based on my idle power test yesterday the V3 needs this to run year round as a solar repeater. This is an estimate of course but would be very close. Unless my V3 is actually faulty and has created its own black hole..lol A setup that works year-round Panel: 100 W, “12 V” nominal (Vmp ≈ 17–19 V) monocrystalline. Charger: MPPT for 1-cell Li-ion (e.g., CN3791 board set to panel MPP) rated ≥2 A. Battery: 1S Li-ion 40,000 mAh (or a 3S LiFePO₄ ~12 Ah with a 5 V buck). Regulator to node: high-efficiency 5 V buck (≥90%, 3 A). Protection: inline fuse, TVS on panel input, Schottky on 5 V rail, decent gauge cable. (edited)
7:54 a.m.
You really want to be looking at the Heltec T114 for a solar repeater..very efficient board mines running on a cheap 5v 3-5w panel and hasn't dropped below 90% charge.. they have built in solar jst port too
1
7:57 a.m.
Obviously there are other boards which are more expensive or cheaper and require more faffing.. If you like a faff the Seeed Xiao nRF52840 board is a beast for efficiency..but needs a separate charger circuit
1

v3's are just for PoC purposes here; we are going to need a solar node, at the highest point around here to get any possibility of meshing with the rest of the nearby nodes, and at that time will be looking for more efficent boards, first I'll need to have some conversations see if I can get permission to place them at the highest points

likkleidiot_nwo 2025-10-10 9:35 a.m.
Update on the V3 power usage at idle.. It's not as bad as I first reported. Looks like when powering via the USB the reported power usage is massive. Powering via battery drops it vastly .I'm new to all this so forgive me if I'm repeating old news. The other boards via usb didn't report this high value like V3. So the runtime on 5000mah battery with 5w 5v solar panel is estimated at 4.2 days. The better news is when I test the V3 in my crows nest carrier board it's more efficient running via battery on there than direct to the board..I'm assuming my charge circuit is a little more efficient. Again totally new to this so any pointers for power testing just let me know
2

it's just the default setting - I've not set it to the max with the db gain on the antenna
6:35 p.m.
I think 10dbm? Although it's set there, I guess the output might be higher after watching Andy's video (edited)
Dayle - M0OUE 2025-10-16 6:36 p.m.
That works out to around 22dbm iirc
pHr0 2025-10-16 6:37 p.m.
Pretty sure that keeps it legal with the duty cycle and antenna gain?
6:38 p.m.
I've it pointed straight at winterhill tho... or as close as I could with my dodgy watch compass lol
tin 2025-10-16 6:39 p.m.
How does 10 work out to be 22 tho? i didn't follow that bit?
pHr0 2025-10-16 6:39 p.m.
I have no idea either - it's all new to me to be honest
tin 2025-10-16 6:40 p.m.
Fair doos! Will try and work it out. But that you have default is good info.
pHr0 2025-10-16 6:40 p.m.
tin - are you getting better connection through the yagi now?
6:41 p.m.
or do you not get choice on the route?
Dayle - M0OUE 2025-10-16 6:42 p.m.
There's an amplifier, it's 10dBm out of the LoRa chip which then goes in to the amp up to 22dBm
pHr0 2025-10-16 6:42 p.m.
ah ok

likkleidiot_nwo 2025-10-18 8:33 a.m.
With a tiny mod the V4 will fit into Alley Cats V3 cases if you are on the look for a V4 case..just need to sand off or mod the edge of the case on the stl so it allows enough room for the battery leads to pull back over the Jst plug...no idea why they didn't have them facing away from the edge of the board (edited)
1
likkleidiot_nwo 2025-10-18 11:07 a.m.

Dayle - M0OUE 2025-10-20 12:45 p.m.
This is the case I use, it keeps the plastic screen protector on the t114 https://makerworld.com/models/652306 (edited)
Remix (Muzi Wurks) Heltec T114 HT2 LoRa Case Remixed by Tinker Kin...
Remix of the Muzi Works H2T Heltec T114 to keep the plastic screen cover. I wanted to be able to have the durability of the cover to assist in protecting the fragile OLED screen in case of a drop. I had to move the screen cutout a smidge (since the original had an insert for the screen cutout without cover) so you can see the screen without bein...

GhostInAShell 2025-10-21 4:04 a.m.
Sorry new to the channel here, and very new to Meshcore. Also apologies if i've missed them somewhere however the top meshcore:// link does not work for me I presume I need the app installed but I am using a t-deck at the moment. What are the settings for the channel\group?
GhostInAShell
Sorry new to the channel here, and very new to Meshcore. Also apologies if i've missed them somewhere however the top meshcore:// link does not work for me I presume I need the app installed but I am using a t-deck at the moment. What are the settings for the channel\group?
Dayle - M0OUE 2025-10-21 4:35 a.m.
Welcome, the top message is just the Winter Hill repeater, if you are in range you'll hear the advert from it eventually. Have you changed your T-Deck to use the narrow settings? If not see below: Scroll to the top, hold the enter button and scroll down to networks, select your current network and change the settings to: frequency:869.617, SF:8, CR:8, BW:62.5. press enter then press the reboot button (the left most one on the bottom edge of the device) (edited)
GhostInAShell 2025-10-21 4:49 a.m.
Hi Yes, it is set to narrow. This was done on setup after flashing last night.
